posts.caption:
Cindy Crawford poses in front of a hollowed tree in St Tropez, like BotticelliвҖҷs Venus; Hiro trains Maria Beadeux to smoke, inspired by the opium dens that he saw as a child growing up in the 1930s; William WegmanвҖҷs 1981 triptych of a dog quietly sat by a pond. Each work currently on display at @hamiltonsgallery as part of its new exhibition, Up Close, tests the distance between subject and lens. вҒ 
вҒ 
Opening in tandem with Photo London and running for the month of May, Up Close is a survey of intimacy, detail and closeness. вҖңWith Photo London on the calendar, we began thinking about how the fair encourages people to get up close to photography,вҖқ Tim Jefferies, Hamiltons gallery founder and principal tells @rose_dodd. вҒ 
вҒ 
Read more at the link in bio рҹ“ІвҒ 
вҒ

posts.caption:
Before The L Word, there was KUTT: the short-lived sister zine to iconoclastic gay magazine BUTT. Lasting for just three issues between 2002 and 2003, the publication took BUTTвҖҷs signature pink pages and tongue-in-cheek attitude and applied them to a survey of the lesbian scene in Amsterdam and beyond.вҒ 
вҒ 
Over two decades on from its original run, copies of the magazine are scarce and in increasingly high demand. Responding to this reignited enthusiasm for the project, the contents of each zine have now been collated in a new book published by @idea.ltd, set to unite this cult title with a fresh generation of readers.вҒ 
вҒ 
вҖңI think younger people are interested in printed matter, itвҖҷs nice to make something printed in amongst this digital overload of images [on social media],вҖқ former editor @jessicagysel tells @meganhwallace. Read more at the link in bio рҹ“ІвҒ 
вҒ 
рҹ“ё Courtesy of KUTT and IDEAвҒ

posts.caption:
When Andrew Bolton first reached out to @sjodwyer and @karolinevitto about the @metmuseumвҖҷs new Costume Institute exhibition, both designers expected to be meeting with an assistant. But each ended up on long, searching calls with Bolton himself вҖ“ a detail that underpins the seriousness with which Costume Art, the inaugural show in the Costume InstituteвҖҷs newly expanded galleries, was conceived. вҒ 
вҒ 
Pairing garments with works from across The MetвҖҷs collection вҖ“ etchings, sculpture, painting вҖ“ the exhibition presents the dressed body as a subject worthy of the same scrutiny as any other art historical question. For OвҖҷDwyer and Vitto, two of the most significant independent designers working with extended sizing in fashion today, being included felt both overdue and, given the current climate, strangely timely. вҒ 
вҒ 
In an exclusive conversation with @mad.e.leiner, the two designers discuss what it means to have this work archived, the words we still havenвҖҷt got right, and the вҖңslow-motion car crashвҖқ of thinning theyвҖҷve both been watching for years. Read more at the link in bio рҹ“ІвҒ 
вҒ

posts.caption:
Over her stellar 20-year career, @phoebejtonkin has captivated audiences. The Sydney-born, New York-based actor began acting as a teenager playing a mermaid in the series вҖҳH2O: Just Add Water,вҖҷ and has since achieved a dynamic array of dramatic parts, a stint in the directorвҖҷs chair and a new role producing and starring in the adaptation of crime thriller, вҖҳThe Dark LakeвҖҷ. While sheвҖҷs relishing the collaborative process, the actor and May 2026 cover star tells BAZAAR, acting remains her first love.вҖңI feel really lucky to be invited into the collaborative process so early on in a project. Usually, as an actor, you turn up and youвҖҷre told, вҖҳThis is what your set looks like, these are your fellow actorsвҖҷ. And with this, I get to be there from the inception of the project and be part of these decisions.вҖқ
Through the link in bio, Phoebe Tonkin reflects on her career and attraction to robust roles that вҖңlean into the complexities of being a woman.вҖқ
Phoebe Tonkin wears @tiffanyandco HardWear collection for the May 2026 issue of HarperвҖҷs BAZAAR Australia/New Zealand.

posts.caption:
вҖңIвҖҷm attracted to things that are authentic and grounded,вҖқ @phoebejtonkin tells BAZAAR of her latest role in the cross-genre вҖҳTwo Years LaterвҖҷ, a post-pandemic romantic dramedy set in Brisbane. Tonkin plays Emily, a woman with a complicated love and family life who sets out on a series of dates with Ryan, played by Brenton Thwaites, to work out if theyвҖҷre destined to marry. вҖңThere was something quite relatable and ordinary about these two people who are in their 30s and trying to navigate dating, coming out of a pandemic, all the isolation and loneliness that theyвҖҷve been dealing with. And I just thought it was really real,вҖқ she shares. It was this funny, quirky love story drama that you donвҖҷt get to do often.вҖқ
Continue reading Phoebe TonkinвҖҷs cover story through the link in bio.

posts.caption:
In an industry where surnames are inherited currencies, Bazaar IndiaвҖҷs April-May cover star, Tabu (@tabutiful), has operated for over three decades on a mononym. To understand her career is to understand what it meant to say yes to things the industry was not ready for and to have the instinct to recognise those things as worthy, even when no template existed for them. But she refuses to call it rebellion. вҖңIt was simply a reflection of who I am. The roles I chose, the decisions I madeвҖ”they came from some similar place throughout my life. Some part of me has been completely unchanged,вҖқ she insists.
Read the full cover story at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
The вҖҳskantвҖҷ is fashionвҖҷs smartest in-between. Seen across runways at Prada, Miu Miu, Loewe, and Bottega Veneta, it has been redefined through clean precision, fluidity, and sculptural layering. ThatвҖҷs really where its strength lies. It doesnвҖҷt ask for much. It builds the outfit for you, with proportion already in place and movement already considered. In the end, it works because it simply lets layering do the thinking.

posts.caption:
The rise of weight-loss drugs is exposing and accelerating a problem fashion has long struggled to solve: sizing systems built for static bodies in a world where peopleвҖҷs bodies are anything but, experts say.
GLP-1 users may have an appetite for shopping, but many of their individual needs can vary significantly. Headlines focused on people simply getting thinner oversimplify reality: widespread GLP-1 use is reshaping bodies in more complex ways.
For fashion firms navigating a broader slowdown вҖ” buoyed by inflation, geopolitical uncertainty and softening demand вҖ” thereвҖҷs a clear opportunity. But itвҖҷs a complicated one to unlock.
BoF's Sheena Butler-Young (@sheena.butler.young) explores fashionвҖҷs new sizing challenge in the age of GLP-1s. Read more #linkinbio
рҹ“· Unsplash

posts.caption:
In 2026, the luxury fashion industry has been placing bets, disproportionately and deliberately, on women athletes.
The relationship between elite female athletes and the fashion and beauty industries is not new: Zheng Qinwen is a Dior ambassador; Iga Swiatek carries LancГҙme. But the architecture of these relationships is changing fast, and in one direction only.
The question becomes: what are those athletes being offered in return?
Olivier Rivard-Cohen argues the framework that turns visibility into brand ownership for top female athletes is almost entirely unbuilt. Read his full opinion piece at the #linkinbio
posts.alt_text: Photo by The Business of Fashion on May 26, 2026. May be a graphic of magazine and text that says '"The debate over whether women's sport generates value has been settled- by the media rights, the fashion houses competing for Met Gala tie-ups, the beauty brands naming arenas. What has not been settled is structural: the layer that transforms a brand deal into an equity position, a career into an institution." OLIVIER RIVARD- RIVARD-COHEN -COHEN STRATEGIC ADVISOR BOF'.

posts.caption: Mined diamonds arenвҖҷt all that rare. ItвҖҷs easy to research how De Beers created this false scarcity through clever marketing and hoarding. The issue is two fold now though: African folks are taking back control of their own resources just as lab diamonds take over and disrupt the whole industry. Coincidence? ItвҖҷs not an easy choice to make - for me, lab grown was the obvious choice at the time that my fiance and I were discussing getting engaged because I had no idea that supply chains could be more traceable and that a long legacy of colonial mine control was coming to an end (and I wanted a larger rock too - nothing gaudy though hehe). I think it comes down to personal choice and taste and everyone needs to make the choice thatвҖҷs right for them. My engagement ring is lab grown while my inherited jewelry from my grandmother obviously contains natural diamonds. I love both вқӨпёҸ
posts.alt_text: Photo by The Business of Fashion on May 26, 2026. May be an image of ring, magazine and text that says 'BOF бһҡбһ•бһӣбһӣ Can Lab- b-Grown Diamonds Move Beyond the Real BeyondtheRealor or Fake' Debate?'.

posts.caption:
This week, Chanel reported its annual results for 2025. Revenue rose 2 percent to $19.3 billion, defying a luxury downturn.
But the number that caught the industryвҖҷs attention wasnвҖҷt the top line вҖ” it was the acceleration. In the second half of the year, ChanelвҖҷs sales grew by high single digits across every category and every region вҖ” and this was before designs by the houseвҖҷs new artistic director, Matthieu Blazy, had even arrived in stores. The excitement alone was enough to change the trajectory.
The momentum has two sources. One is Blazy, whose runway debut last October has sparked what the industry is calling вҖңBlazymaniaвҖқ, a level of excitement Chanel hasnвҖҷt seen in years. The other is the leadership of chief executive Leena Nair, who has spent her tenure investing heavily in ChanelвҖҷs retail network, its manufacturing, and its people вҖ” building the foundations that allowed a creative spark to catch fire.
Two years before they would work together at Chanel, Blazy and Nair spoke at #BoFVOICES 2023. Separately they each made the case for craft, people and long-term thinking over speed and scale.
This week on The BoF Podcast, we revisit their talks to understand how their synergistic philosophies are powering ChanelвҖҷs newfound momentum.
рҹҺҷпёҸ Listen now #linkinbio #BoFPodcast
рҹ“· Getty Images

posts.caption:
Hi this is @ImranAmed, welcome to my weekly letter.
On the surface, there was plenty to celebrate at the Financial TimesвҖҷ Business of Luxury Summit this week. Delphine Arnault and Jonathan Anderson marked one year of their joint effort to re-energise Dior. StГ©phane de La Faverie, chief executive of The EstГ©e Lauder Companies, underscored the beauty conglomerateвҖҷs nine consecutive months of growth after four years of decline. And Victoria Beckham said both her fashion and beauty divisions are now profitable after almost two decades of losses.
But beneath the public relations spin, a reckoning is underway as the luxury industry struggles to recover from a punishing downturn in demand.
When asked what is the greatest risk facing the luxury industry, BainвҖҷs Claudia DвҖҷArpizio didnвҖҷt cite tariffs, the war in the Middle East or slow growth in China, but rising inequality: the risk that luxury goods are becoming markers of exclusion rather than totems of aspiration.
This is a dynamic The Business of Fashion has been tracking for at least a decade. That risk has only increased amid the rise of AmericaвҖҷs tech centibillionaires, AsiaвҖҷs family empires and the global hedge fund class. Contrary to popular belief, вҖңold moneyвҖқ doesnвҖҷt drive the luxury sector: The majority of luxury growth comes from aspirants and the upwardly mobile вҖ” you get a better life and you mark the occasion and signal your newfound status by buying something beautiful. But when wealth polarisation becomes so extreme that the ladder itself feels broken, aspiration melts away. People stop admiring the wealthy, and start resenting them.
According to research by Bain, the luxury market lost 50 million customers between 2022 and 2024, shrinking from 400 million to 350 million globally, amid macroeconomic gloom and extravagant price increases. вҖңVery important customersвҖқ вҖ” the top 2 percent of spenders вҖ” now account for 45 percent of all luxury purchases, up from 35 percent in 2021, while millions of shoppers have walked away.
So, how did we end up here? Read my full letter at the #linkinbio

posts.caption:
one of my truly fav collections of all time - Christopher John Rogers Fall 2020 рҹ’ӣ The whole collection is a swarm of colors with each more exciting than the previous one. Trash bags was one of the inspirations of this glorious collection along with Madame GrГЁs, pink and orange carryout bags, and monochromatic church wear. The set design by Sauras + Garriga was old-school glam meets cozy comfort, an absolute beauty.
CJRвҖҷs extravagant silhouettes included exaggerated sleeves, cinched waists, dramatic skirts, big shoulders, and ruffled collars. The colors in this collection, Magnificent. Orange, yellow, olive, neon, violet, fuchsia, red, maroons, pink; all of them on acid times a hundred. We saw promising tailoring in some gleaming suits and some intense monochromatic color-blocked suits in shades of yellow and red. Each look here was better than the next, among these came a big blazing brilliant teal gown.This collection will forever have a place in my heart, something about it connects very deeply within.
It reminded me how powerful fashion can feel when it fully commits to joy, color, and drama without looking forced. everything felt alive, from the silhouettes to the styling. it had that rare energy where you just enjoy the clothes for what they are.

posts.caption:
When she was four, the artist Widline Cadet (@widline_ ) was separated from her mother for six years as she emigrated from Haiti to New York to pursue a better life for her family.
At 10 years old, Cadet reunited with her mother in New York, but as she grew into adulthood, she realized that she didn't know her well at all. Nor did Cadet have a larger sense of her family, the ancestral threads that weave back through time.
Photographs of her own childhood and family were scarce. Her mother didn't have a picture of her own mother. Memories faded with each passing year.
For nearly a decade, Cadet has been crafting her own "living archive," exploring the diasporic experience and the elusiveness of memory. The largest presentation of her work to date is on view at @milwaukeeart.
рҹ“ё: Widline Cadet

In @bootsrileyвҖҷs @iloveboostersmovie, color serves as its own character, fueling the full-throttle chaos of his latest eccentric comedy about a group of girlfriends who steal and resellвҖ”or "boost"вҖ”clothes to make ends meet.
Starring @keke, @naomiackie, @taylour вҖ“ who play Corvette, Sade and Mariah in the Velvet Gang вҖ” and @demimoore, who serves as the captivating retail mogul-villain Christie Smith, Riley's sophomore feature is a raucous critique of over-consumption and, at times, a sobering attack on the fashion industry's ethical failings.
The film's costumes вҖ“ which evoke a Bay-area-grunge-meets-Gen-Z-raver type of vibe вҖ“ are the colorful concoction of Oscar-nominated costume designer @shirleykurata.
At the link in bio, read how Kurata worked alongside Riley to craft the wild, neon-drenched world of 'Boosters'.

At the Frick Museum on Wednesday night, @louisvuitton introduced FranГ§ois BoucherвҖҷs putti to Keith HaringвҖҷs radiant babies.
For the labelвҖҷs Cruise 2027 collection, staged in the hallowed halls of the @frickcollection a New York City Gilded Age mansion-turned-art gallery, designer @nicolasghesquiere created several pieces adorned with works by the late US pop artist Keith Haring.
HaringвҖҷs trademark faceless figures dominated satin tops, dresses, bags, leather jackets and high-top sneakers. вҖңPop art, pop culture and pop luxury,вҖқ read the brandвҖҷs accompanying press release. вҖңThe notion of popular as a powerful medium, the conveyance of messages to all.вҖқ

posts.caption:
At @festivaldecannes on Wednesday, supermodel @bellahadid attended the premiere of Antonin Baudry's new Charles de Gaulle biopic, "La Bataille de Gaulle," in a form-fitting ivory gown by Schiaparelli.
From its trompe l'Е“il lace embroidery to the dark brooch holding everything together, the French couture house's inspiration was clear: the crochet floral gown that late style icon Jane Birkin wore to the Union of the Artists gala in Paris in 1969.
In contrast to Hadid's picture-perfect styling, Birkin's outfit had resulted from a last-minute wardrobe fix вҖ” one that epitomized her gloriously blithe approach to fashion.
Believing her neckline to be too high, the English socialite decided to wear her @emiliopucci dress backwards. In doing so, she turned a backless gown into a daringly plunging one.
Almost six decades later, moments of such sartorial insouciance have all but disappeared from the red carpet. But let this not be a criticism of Hadid, her longtime stylist mimicuttrell or the gown's creator, @schiaparelli creative director @danielroseberry, who together created an outfit befitting of the moment.
рҹ“ё : Lyvans Boolaky/Getty Images/Samir Hussein/WireImage/Keystone-France/Gamma-Keystone/

What does it take to build a cult-favorite fashion brand as a teenagerвҖ”without a design background, without investors, and without anyone fully taking you seriously? For sisters @brendaliang and @clarahychi, founders of @sirenbasics, the answer was an unshakeable belief in what they were building.
Born from a simple desire for playful, beautiful underwear that didnвҖҷt demand a luxury price tag, Siren Basics has grown into one of this generationвҖҷs most beloved intimates brands (co-signed by the likes of @britneyspears, @devonleecarlson, and @treaclychild). We sat down with the duo to talk about the вҖңunsexyвҖқ reality of building a business and why underwear deserves to be taken seriously at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
Honestly, I donвҖҷt understand why itвҖҷs almost always the same girls, wearing the same outfits, the white t-shirt, the thong sandals, the same minimalist aesthetic, who automatically get to dictate whatвҖҷs considered fashionable.
And I donвҖҷt mean this as criticism toward people who dress that way.
What surprises me is that, despite the incredible diversity of bodies, styles, and genuinely original, creative, and far more daring fashion ideas out there, the conversation still seems to revolve around the same aesthetic formula.

posts.caption:
In вҖңDream your paintings, paint your dreams!вҖқ Tim Walker turns fashion into a surreal art installation inspired by the distorted figures and emotional intensity of Egon Schiele. The editorial feels less like a traditional fashion story and more like stepping inside a painting thatвҖҷs constantly shifting between fantasy, performance and psychological theatre.
What makes Tim WalkerвҖҷs work so recognizable is the way he approaches fashion photography almost like world building. The clothes are important, but atmosphere always comes first. In this story, twisted body language, exaggerated silhouettes and theatrical staging create images that feel strange, playful and slightly unsettling at the same time. Walker has always understood that the strongest editorials are the ones that completely transport you somewhere else instead of simply showing beautiful clothing.
The Schiele inspiration works especially well because the editorial never tries to imitate fine art too literally. Instead, it translates Expressionism into fashion imagery through movement, distortion and emotion. Kiki Willems and James Crewe almost look like living brushstrokes inside the set, while Jacob KвҖҷs styling pushes the silhouettes further into this surreal, off-balanced world. (fashionCOWпҝј)
What also makes the story stand out years later is how physical and handmade everything feels. In a time where so much fashion imagery becomes overly retouched or digitally polished, Tim WalkerвҖҷs editorials still feel tactile, theatrical and deeply human. ThatвҖҷs probably why his work continues to feel timeless while so many campaigns disappear after one season.
Editorial Credits

posts.caption:
What makes this British Vogue story so powerful is the emotional weight behind every image. Editorials often try to imitate intimacy, but this one feels real. You can immediately sense the connection between Anok Yai and her mother, Nyibol Akuei, without the images needing to explain themselves. ThereвҖҷs a softness, protection and familiarity in the way they move around each other that makes the story feel much bigger than a normal fashion editorial.
Anok has spoken many times about how close she is to her mother, especially after her recent health struggles. In the Vogue interview, Nyibol describes how painful it was seeing her daughter in the hospital and how deeply connected they are as a family. That emotional closeness completely changes the energy of the editorial. It stops feeling like вҖңfashion imagesвҖқ and starts feeling archival вҖ” almost like family history documented through luxury fashion photography. (British Vogueпҝј)
Visually, the editorial works because Rafael Pavarotti understands how to photograph Black skin with depth, richness and drama without flattening it into something overly polished. The lighting feels cinematic and spiritual at the same time. Every frame has scale, but the relationship between mother and daughter always remains the center of attention. That balance is incredibly difficult to achieve in fashion photography.
Anok Yai has always had one of the strongest presences in modern modeling because she understands narrative. She doesnвҖҷt just model clothes вҖ” she creates atmosphere. And here, that atmosphere feels deeply personal. The editorial quietly speaks about migration, family, survival, beauty and legacy without becoming performative or overexplained. ThatвҖҷs what separates a great editorial from a forgettable campaign: it leaves behind emotion, not just visuals.
Publication вҖ” British Vogue
Model вҖ” Anok Yai
Featuring вҖ” Nyibol Akuei

posts.caption:
This summerвҖҷs style mood feels less clean and much more personal. The best outfits right now look slightly chaotic, undone and thrown together in the best way, tiny shorts with oversized shirts, ballet flats with sportswear, messy layering, and vintage accessoires.
Sleaze and old runway styling, but styled in a more playful way. From Kate Moss-denim micro shorts and slouchy belts to colorful tights, fitted tanks, embellished shorts and oversized graphic tees, the focus is less about looking вҖңperfectвҖқ and more about having character.
Even the more dressed-up looks still feel authentic. Smudged sunglasses, worn-in denim, oversized bags, ballet shoes, tiny gym shorts or random statement pieces make the outfits feel human instead of overstyled. This summer is really about mixing glamour with casualness, looking like you didnвҖҷt try too hard while still creating a fun silhouette and attitude.

posts.caption:
In Athletic Girl for Vogue Italia August 2016, Steven Klein turns fashion photography into a staged power fantasy. The editorial plays with dominance, sexuality and control, placing Lara Stone at the center of every image more like a character than a model. She doesnвҖҷt just wear the clothes, she completely controls the atmosphere of the story.
What makes the editorial so memorable is how fully it leans into that idea. The exaggerated poses, glossy styling and theatrical tension give the images a very specific energy that still feels striking years later. Lara Stone brings this raw, almost intimidating presence to the story, while Patti WilsonвҖҷs styling pushes everything further into this hyper-glamorous, slightly surreal world.
ItвҖҷs the kind of editorial that understands fashion photography should create a mood, not just present clothes.
Editorial Credits

posts.caption:
What makes the Zara x Bad Bunny collaboration feel so strange is the contradiction behind it. Over the past years, Benito built an image around political awareness, authenticity and cultural resistance. He positioned himself as an artist who speaks for marginalized communities and distances himself from the polished corporate machinery dominating both music and fashion.
That is exactly why this partnership feels so cynical.
Zara is not just another fashion brand. It is one of the largest fast fashion corporations in the world, built on mass production, aggressive consumption and the commercialization of identities. Watching an artist whose image is so closely tied to rebellion and anti establishment messaging suddenly become the face of that machine inevitably raises questions about how performative celebrity activism really is once global corporations get involved.
And the collaboration becomes even harder to defend creatively because the collection itself feels painfully uninspired. Oversized basics, washed tones, predictable silhouettes and a campaign aesthetic that looks indistinguishable from regular Zara menswear. There is nothing here that reflects the risk taking or visual boldness that made Bad Bunny culturally interesting in the first place.
Without BenitoвҖҷs name attached to it, the collection would most likely disappear into the endless cycle of forgettable Zara drops within a week.
Of course, it would also be dishonest to pretend most celebrities would reject a collaboration like this. Fashion has always been tied to capitalism and almost every major artist eventually becomes absorbed into the same corporate structures they once appeared to criticize.
But with Bad Bunny, the contradiction feels especially glaring because so much of his appeal came from the idea that he was different from exactly this kind of system.

posts.caption:
This collection understood clothing as character.
David Koma Pre-Fall 2025 has that thing where the clothes donвҖҷt just look good, they immediately tell you who the person wearing them might be.
ThereвҖҷs a lot happening here: sheer bodysuits, fur collars, red tights, sharp heels, leather, denim, long black silhouettes, strange proportions. But it still feels controlled. Nothing looks random, even when the shapes are dramatic.
The collection plays with a very specific kind of glamour. Not soft, not romantic, not too clean. More like someone dressing up to be watched, but still keeping a bit of distance.
The fur pieces make the body look framed. The red looks feel almost graphic. The sheer pieces keep things light, but never weak. Even the denim and leather moments still belong to the same world.
ThatвҖҷs what makes it interesting. ItвҖҷs not just a set of nice looks. It feels like a cast of characters.
Credits:
Collection: David Koma Pre-Fall 2025
Designer: David Koma

posts.caption:
In 2023, women made up 80 per cent of the book-buying market in the UK, US and Canada. In the US, the gender gap in reading has persisted for years, with fiction-reading among men facing an especially precipitous decline (if there were any real takeaways from last yearвҖҷs вҖңperformative maleвҖқ obsession, it was that men who read now feel like such an anomaly that spotting one in the wild is cause for suspicion.) вҒ 
вҒ 
With the reading gap between men and women continuing to widen, itвҖҷs not surprising that reading gap couples exist. But how much should these gaps actually matter in dating, and does a literary mismatch really have to be a dealbreaker?вҒ

posts.caption:
Heatwaves turn Britain feral. Under a blazing sun, rules dissolve, inhibitions melt, and everyone starts acting slightly unhinged. Toxic exes get texted, strangers get kissed, balconies become hookup zones, and breakfast somehow becomes a Calippo and a Pornstar Martini. вҒ 
вҒ 
From Atonement to Love Island, heat has always been the backdrop to chaos, lust, and bad decisions. Scientists say sunshine boosts serotonin and lowers inhibitions, but British heat adds a special kind of madness: humid, sticky, restless energy that makes people feel reckless and wildly alive. Maybe itвҖҷs heat brain, maybe itвҖҷs climate doom вҖ” either way, the hotter it gets, the more feral we become.вҒ 
вҒ

posts.caption:
A decade or two ago, it seemed like everyone wanted to be famous. The reality TV golden era of the 2000s saw the rise of shows like Big Brother, BritainвҖҷs Got Talent and The X Factor, which offered an unprecedented platform for вҖҳordinaryвҖҷ people to take a shot at becoming a star overnight вҖ“ and it was a shot many people took. вҒ 
вҒ 
Today, however, young people seem more ambivalent about fame. While one 2010 survey found that over half of 16-year-olds wanted to be famous in lieu of a career вҖ“ with more than a fifth planning to achieve this by appearing on a reality TV show вҖ“ new YouGov research published this month found that just nine per cent of Gen Z want to be famous, with a paltry five aspiring to become an influencer, and 79 per cent saying they would вҖңprefer a private lifeвҖқ. But when did fame shift from aspirational to undesirable вҖ“ and why?вҒ 
вҒ

Coral jewellery is quietly returning to luxury fashion conversations.
A recent couture appearance featuring coral jewellery sparked renewed attention around a material long associated with heritage luxury, Mediterranean aesthetics, and vintage glamour.
In a market increasingly driven by colour, individuality, and expressive styling, coral offers something visually distinct from traditional diamond-heavy jewellery.
But coral also remains one of luxuryвҖҷs most debated materials вҖ” sitting between craftsmanship, rarity, sustainability, and ethical sourcing conversations.
The bigger question is:
Could coral jewellery actually make a stronger comeback in 2026?
Save this post.
Share it with your team.
And tell us вҖ” would coral work in todayвҖҷs luxury market?

posts.caption:
We could go see Boop!
Quinta Brunson is set to develop and star as Betty Boop in a new film by her production company Fifth Chance Productions, alongside creator Max FleischerвҖҷs grandson Mark Fleischer and Fleischer Studios.
According to the filmвҖҷs press release, the story will trace BettyвҖҷs origin and evolution through Max FleischerвҖҷs perspective, digging into the вҖңrelationship between the artist and his creation as he navigates the creative and commercial pressures of building one of the worldвҖҷs first animated icons, particularly as that icon begins to take on a life of its own.вҖқ
Online reactions to Brunson stepping into the role are split. Some fans see the two-time Emmy winner as a perfect fit for the characterвҖҷs reboot, while others are questioning her casting.
Betty BoopвҖҷs influence and origins have long-sparked debate. While Fleischer claimed to have based the character on a dog, singer Helen Kane famously sued the studio, arguing Boop was modeled after her. In court, the studio pointed the characterвҖҷs iconic baby voice to Black performers of the 1920s, including Baby Esther, a popular child star.
BettyвҖҷs roots have been debatable for decades, but either way, sheвҖҷs a fictional character and Brunson is a great choice for the legendary diva!
Swipe through for more of her fascinating history.
#bettyboop #quintabrunson #film #history #animation

posts.caption:
After years of soft neutrals, skin tints, and вҖңno-makeup makeupвҖқ dominating the algorithm, Marc Jacobs Beauty is relaunching on its own terms with statement shades and party balloon-inspired packaging.
The brand first debuted in 2013 and quickly reached cult status before disappearing in 2021 after its partnership with Kendo ended. Now it returns under a new license with Coty Inc., re-entering a market that has fundamentally shifted.
While вҖңclean girlвҖқ minimalism has dominated beauty since the brand shuttered, the cultural pendulum seems to be swinging towards color and maximalism, and away from former mega-brands like Glossier.
Marc Jacobs Beauty is returning with saturated pigments, high-gloss finishes, and sculptural packaging in a relaunch Jacobs calls a celebration of вҖңcreativity and self-expression.вҖқ
The rollout includes seven hero products priced from $26 to $42, reviving fan favorites like its best-selling gel liners in 21 shades, alongside new products like a multi-use blush stick.
Whether this return meaningfully disrupts the beauty category remains to be seen, but itвҖҷs clearly not trying to blend in.
Marc Jacobs Beauty drops May 28 on marcjacobs.com, followed by a June 1 release atВ Sephora.
#marcjacobs #beauty #makeup #marcjacobsbeauty #sephora

posts.caption:
On May 19th, Jonathan Andic was arrested in connection to the death of his father Isak Andic, the billionaire founder of Mango, who died in 2024 after slipping into a ravine on a Montserrat hiking trail. Authorities have outlined new evidence against Jonathan, prompting his arrest as a homicide suspect.
Jonathan, the only witness to his fatherвҖҷs death, claimed that he was a few meters ahead on the hiking trail when his father paused to take photos of the scenery. He told emergency services that he heard the fall, but did not see it occur. He also claimed that his relationship with his father was good, despite known tensions around JonathanвҖҷs business failings at Mango in 2015.
However, new evidence shows that IsakвҖҷs cell phone was found in his pocket at the time of his death, and no photos appear on his camera roll of the hike. As well, the autopsy report found no scuffs on IsakвҖҷs hands, suggesting that he fell backward, not forward, into the ravine. Examination of IsakвҖҷs shoes also showed that the soles were worn down вҖңdeliberatelyвҖқ to create the appearance of a slip. Jonathan was also found to have visited the hiking trail multiple times shortly before his fatherвҖҷs death, causing investigators to suspect premeditation.
Most importantly, JonathanвҖҷs tense relationship with his father was illuminated by EstefanГӯa Knuth, the professional golfer and long-term girlfriend of Isak (who happens to be in a legal dispute with IsakвҖҷs children for вӮ¬70m of the inheritance.) KnuthвҖҷs claims are supported by WhatsApp messages between Jonathan and Isak which reveal the sonвҖҷs вҖңobsession with moneyвҖқ and his demands for a living inheritance, which Isak was вҖңforcedвҖқ to agree to.
Finally, in 2024, Jonathan learned that Isak had planned to set up a вҖңfoundation to help people in need.вҖқ WhatsApp messages show a shift in the sonвҖҷs attitude towards his father, causing him to express a desire to reconcile their differences. Thus, Jonathan invited Isak on the hike in Montserrat, just the two of them, to вҖңtalk alone.вҖқ Wanting to recover his relationship with his son, Isak accepted the invitation that would lead to his untimely death, and the foundation was never realized.

According to the president, the new ballroom is also a new security flex for additional construction underneath it. вҖңThe ballroom becomes a shield for everything that you see here,вҖқ he said, describing it as вҖңthe one opportunity for the military to do something.вҖқ Guess the bunker theories were right all along.
The presidentвҖҷs comments come as Republicans struggle to pass nearly $1B in funding for ballroom security and other WH projects, despite Trump repeatedly insisting his passion project would not be paid for by taxpayers (shocking, right?)

posts.caption:
As AI looms over job prospects, itвҖҷs no surprise it has become an unwelcome topic at this yearвҖҷs college commencementsвҖҰ well, at least for new grads anyway.
Two viral videos emerged over the past week showing graduation speakers gloating about the future of AI. Gloria Caulfield, a real estate exec, went viral after she looked visibly shocked as students booed her May 8 commencement speech at the University of Central Florida after comparing AI to the industrial revolution. вҖңWhat happened?,вҖқ she asked. вҖңI struck a nerve.вҖқ
That same energy popped up again at the University of Arizona, where former Google CEO Eric Schmidt delivered a similar message last Friday, but even more aggressively. вҖңIf you donвҖҷt care about science thatвҖҷs okay,вҖқ he stated, вҖңbecause AI is gonna touch everything else as well.вҖқ Schmidt reportedly anticipated backlash from students following allegations of rape and sexual harassment, and surveillance, filed in a lawsuit last November.
Online, the comments are basically echoing the same vibe. вҖңArenвҖҷt commencement addresses supposed to be motivating?,вҖқ one Threads user wrote. вҖңWhy do they keep sending AI simps to college graduations to brag to them about how AI is going to steal their jobs?,вҖқ another asked.
At Glendale Community College, dozens of graduate names were reportedly skipped and/or mispronounced by an AI reader replacing traditional announcers.
College president Dr. Tiffany Hernandez laughed off the tech issue, saying, вҖңEach of you were able to walk the stage and get a picture, which is what I would hope is the most meaningful,вҖқ she said. вҖңI am so sorry. ThereвҖҷs plenty of opportunities, I hope, to take some really good pictures and to celebrate you with your loved ones as well.вҖқ
Maybe AI just isnвҖҷt needed in a tradition thatвҖҷs been doing fine for hundreds of years without it.
#graduation #2026 #jobs #news #reels

posts.caption:
Everlane has officially sold to Shein, the fast-fashion giant, in light of the companyвҖҷs $90m debt burden.
The capsule-wardrobe retailer was founded in 2011, promising sustainability and вҖңradical transparencyвҖқ in their pricing and supply chain. Their target customer was a coming-of-age millennial who was eager for affordable fashion that aligned with their value systems. For years, Everlane remained the de facto workplace-casual retailer for that very customer, offering simple, non-branded, trend resistant options which supposedly didnвҖҷt come from sweatshops and took a bullheaded approach to cutting carbon emissions.
You could say that Everlane and brands like it defined the 2010s, to some extent. Everlane was a leader in a decade of optimistic retailers, each of which sold its own tailored promise of creating a better world through DTC sales; Reformation sold ethical occasion dresses, All Birds sold sneakers made of recycled materials, Outdoor Voices sold inclusive athleisure, and so on. The millennial utopia was, it seems, an ethical value proposition that consumers and investors could get behind.
But now, many of these brands are falling apart under the crushing truth of capitalism: Ethical consumption may not be as realistic on the large-scale as weвҖҷd hoped it could be. Everlane is, afterall, being acquired by the very force of retail that it was supposed to replace, one that would sooner churn out desirable styles and exploit consumersвҖҷ growing interest in hyper-trends and luxury than worry over the environment or supply chain problems like slave labour.
What does that mean for customers who relied on Everlane to provide a cohesive wardrobe sans effort, anxiety, or guilt? One Reddit post asks: вҖңWhat are we buying/wearing to replace Everlane?вҖқ The responses are bleak, offering little in terms of replacements and much in terms of disappointment.
вҖңIвҖҷm just so sick of everything. I just want quality clothing thatвҖҷs not made from child labor and has ethical/sustainable production lines,вҖқ one user said.
Can a brand like that exist anymore? Or is the dream of the 2010s dead?

posts.caption:
The 2026 #Cannes Film Festival has come and gone, and so has the seemingly endless parade of red-carpet looks. Two weeks of fashion from the Croisette gave way to some key trends, most prevalentlyвҖ”and thus strikinglyвҖ”the sheer number of naked dresses worn in spite of the festival's attempt to ban the style over the past two years. (Look no further than @bellahadid or @daisyedgarjones, both of whom incorporated see-through fabrics into their appearances.)
Elsewhere, we saw an embrace of refined tailoring, while other stars welcomed tried-and-true cinematic glamourвҖ”with some exciting pops of color and structure from the likes of @demimoore and @cateblanchett. While some people are sweating in the newest workout classes, others are going back to the basics.

posts.caption:
At some point, buying concert tickets stopped feeling exciting and started feeling like psychological warfare. Queues that freeze. Dynamic pricing that punishes actual fans. Presales hidden behind credit cards and memberships. Resale tickets that cost more than international flights. After missing out on The WeekndвҖҷs Bangkok show despite spending two hours in a queue, Abhya Adlakha (@abhyaaa) traces how concerts became less about fandom and more about surviving the system around it.
Read the full story at the link in bio.
#EsquireIndia #TheWeeknd
posts.alt_text: Photo by Esquire India on May 26, 2026. May be an image of magazine, poster and text that says 'Recently, I waited two hours for a Weeknd ticket I never got. I logged in early for the Bangkok presale of his final After Hours Til Dawn Asia tour, then spent an hour entering the queue and another watching a frozen progress bar promise I was "almost there." When the site finally let me in, every section greyed out before I could even finish entering my card details. After fifteen years of fandom, the closest] I came to seeing The Weeknd live was a Cloudflare loading screen. Abhya AbhyaAdlakha ОөПҖОұОјОҪОө INDIA'.

posts.caption:
вҖңHi Shakaila weвҖҷre xyz producers and weвҖҷd love to make a tv show with youвҖқ IвҖҷve had a handful of those conversations over the years and they quickly go from рҹӨ© to рҹ«©
I used to feel disheartened until I looked around and saw so many examples of people not waiting for permission and instead deciding to get up and make shit happen! So thatвҖҷs what I decided to do, turn this decades long dream into a reality! I pulled in every favour I had! Turned my friends into producers and show runners, begged and borrowed and you know what, it turned out pretty damn good if I do say so myself рҹҳ…
This black history month especially, IвҖҷm inspired by the talented Black British women who have shown me that itвҖҷs possible to make a mark on the small screen no matter the limitations!
I absolutely loved makeover shows growing up! But as I got older I saw how they were more brutal than beautiful!
Today, fashion psychology shows us just how deeply our clothes impact our mental health, identity, and confidence.
ThatвҖҷs why IвҖҷm so excited to share The Wearapy Show my new makeover series that blends fashion psychology, emotional healing and real style transformations.
We help everyday people reclaim their wardrobes after major life shifts: grief, motherhood, identity struggles, and more.
With a team of experts and no judgment, just science-backed guidance and love.
Episodes 1 and 2 launch 26th October on the Fashion is Psychology YouTube Channel. Subscribe now and follow the journey.
#bhm #wearapy #fashionpsychology #makeover

posts.caption:
HUGE NEWS: Good On You has officially rated over 7,000 brands рҹҺүВ This is a major accomplishment, as even more brandsвҖ”from the small and indie through the large and luxuryвҖ”are in our Directory.вҒ 
вҒ 
What goes into ratings? рҹӨ” A lot, actually: we have a rigorous, comprehensive system analysing up to 1,000 data points across 100 key issues to arrive at our lowest score (вҖңWe AvoidвҖқ) to our highest score (вҖңGreatвҖқ). Ratings are based solely on a brandвҖҷs transparent, public reporting that we pair with credible third-party indices, certifications, and standards.вҒ 
вҒ 
How do we select brands to rate? WellвҖҰwith your help рҹҘ№ Many of our ratings come through community requests, shared in comments and messages, which helps us understand which brands to prioritise to meet this moment. Additionally, our retail and ecommerce partners along with brands request ratings too, which is a key means to apply change to the industry from within.вҒ 
вҒ 
Another item to celebrate here? Of these rated brands, 1,000 have received our two highest scores, вҖңGoodвҖқ and вҖңGreatвҖқ. This is very exciting news, representative of how more brands are doing considering their impact on workers, the planet, and animals. (Even if that also meansвҖҰ6000 brands have a lot more to do to reduce their impact.)вҒ 
вҒ 
HereвҖҷs to continuing to keep people like you informed while supporting more sustainable brands that are making the difference! Cheers рҹҘӮ Explore our 7,000 ratings at the link too вңЁвҒ 
вҒ 
#SustainableFashion #FastFashion #ConsciousConsumer #EthicalConsumer #SustainableStyle #ConsciousStyle #GoodOnYou #GoodOnYouApp #EthicalFashionвҒ

posts.caption:
Meet the top rated brands of 2025 рҹҺҠвҒ 
вҒ 
In the past year, we rated more than 1500 brands and are proud to share the ten that received our highest marks, illustrating a commitment to more sustainable practices that protect people, the planet, and animals рҹ’ҡвҒ 
вҒ 
WhatвҖҷs a theme that unites these brands? рҹ‘Җ Outside of a commitment to reducing their impact, these brands are all small and independent, a reminder of where to look in the industry for leadership and innovation. If youвҖҷre looking for a reminder to support small brands this year, this is it.вҒ 
вҒ 
Cheers to all these brands, along with all the brands that were rated вҖңGoodвҖқ and вҖңGreatвҖқ in the past year! HereвҖҷs to continuing to make a difference and for championing a better approach to what we wear. posts.caption:

вҒ 
A decade may seem short but, in reflecting on all the work weвҖҷve done in the past decade, weвҖҷre in awe of everything that has transpiredвҖ”and are grateful for the hard work of the current and former team members along with you.вҒ 
вҒ 
Whether youвҖҷve shared our posts or have been a partner in this business, the work of creating a more sustainable future isnвҖҷt done alone but in partnership with others. posts.caption:
No one wants to wear plastic. And yet: so many of us are рҹҳўвҒ 
вҒ 
From a report we conducted last year, echoed by the 2024 Circularity Gap Report, the vast majority of materials being made are syntheticsвҖ”which means most clothes being made come from synthetics рҹҳөвҒ 
вҒ 
Synthetics do not biodegrade and shed microplastics when washed. They are an environmental disaster, all of which is propelled by being fast fashionвҖҷs material of choice. posts.caption:
GisГЁle Pelicot has responded to news that, following the rape of two girls in Hampshire, a judge has spared three teenage boys of custodial sentences. Pelicot, who became a feminist icon when she waived her anonymity as the survivor of multiple rapes, told the BBC she was вҖҳdeeply shocked that these individuals were in fact able to gain their freedom again when in fact the victims are suffering so hard they will never be able to healвҖҷ.
One of the victims of the Hampshire attacks has said the decision to give the boys youth rehabilitation sentences was like вҖҳa rock straight in my faceвҖҷ. Pelicot applauded her for speaking out: вҖҳI really salute her strength and the decision she made, because I know that itвҖҷs an incredibly difficult decision.вҖҷ The sentences are to be reviewed by the attorney general.
posts.alt_text: Photo by Grazia UK on May 26, 2026. May be a doodle of text that says 'Rape is a crime and justice has an essential role[...] to recognise the suffering of victims, and to remember that [it] must not remain unpunished. GISГҲLE PELICOT GRAZIA'.

And if Arsenal fans didnвҖҷt already have plenty to celebrate right now, the campaign adds further star power courtesy of midfielder Declan Rice and Lioness Leah Williamson, alongside a cast of British pop culture favourites including Lucy Punch, Romeo Beckham, Jodie Turner-Smith, Rosie Huntington-Whiteley, Stephen Graham and Eberechi Eze.
Football, fashion and a heatwave? Very British, really.

@kith and @leomessi link up with @adidasfootball to mark two milestones at once: KithвҖҷs 15th anniversary and MessiвҖҷs 20 years since his international debut. вҒ 
вҒ 
The result is a collection that sits right between performance and lifestyle, pairing on-pitch essentials with everyday tailored and cut-and-sew pieces.вҒ 
вҒ 
Across footwear, goalkeeper jerseys, suits, denim jackets, tracksuits, and more, the drop moves seamlessly between match-day function and off-duty ease. ItвҖҷs a wardrobe built for both sides of the game, where stadium-ready gear and casual staples share the same language, anchored by MessiвҖҷs legacy, KithвҖҷs design lens, and adidas' know-how and resources. The collection is slated to be released on May 29 via Kith.

posts.caption:
It's giving Cher.вҒ 
вҒ 
@alysaxliu brought full rockstar energy to the 2026 American Music Awards, stepping onto the red carpet in a custom all-black @chromeheartsofficial look layered with standout jewelry. The Olympic gold medalist stacked heavy silver chains, rings, and statement accessories across the fit, pushing the dark, punk-inspired styling even further with a silver bridge piercing and matching metallic manicure details designed to resemble facial jewelry.вҒ 
вҒ 
Presenting the award for Best Rock/Alternative Artist, Liu fully embraced the rising wave of edgy Gen Z beauty trends. Whether the bridge piercing is permanent or not, the Chrome-heavy styling and rebellious details cemented the look as one of the nightвҖҷs boldest fashion moments.

Martin Margiela is opening his personal archives to the public. The designer is set to auction off pieces from his own fashion memorabilia collection after years of ownership.вҒ 
вҖңAfter many years of moving my archives from place to place, and lending some pieces for exhibitions, I felt it was time to let go of part of my fashion memorabilia.вҖқ вҖ” Martin Margiela
The live auction will take place on July 9, 2026, with a public exhibition running from July 4вҖ“8 at Maurice Auction. A catalogue will be released soon on @maurice_auction

posts.caption:
For all their coding genius and super-efficient microchips, tech giants still canвҖҷt quite crack swag.вҒ 
вҒ 
So when Silicon Valley wants to sell camera-equipped AI glasses, it calls in fashion people to make them look less painfully tech-brained. Meta figured this out first, partnering with Ray-Ban in 2019 to remake its iconic Wayfarers and later extending the formula to OakleyвҖҷs sporty, accidentally stylish performance shades. Now Google and Samsung are entering the race, with Apple reportedly not far behind.вҒ 
вҒ 
Instead of linking with eyewear heavyweights, @google and @samsung tapped @gentlemonster, the South Korean label responsible for some of fashionвҖҷs strangest and best futuristic frames. The result is a surprisingly sleek pair of all-black cat-eye smart glasses arriving this fall. Samsung supplies the discreet built-in cameras while Google adds Gemini AI features like navigation, translation, and messaging. Basically, they do all the usual smart-glasses tricks but finally look like something people might actually want to wear.

вҒ 
Founder Teddy Santis brings ALDвҖҷs elevated menswear and his love for NYC street culture to the region for a six-month residency in the UAE. Mirroring the nostalgic aesthetic of the iconic Mulberry St. and London flagships, the bespoke space delivers the brandвҖҷs entire world, including its signature CafГ© Leon Dore concept and an authentic AimГ© Leon Dore Porsche 911SC on the shop floor. Elsewhere, a signature sports-centric sculpture by artist Tyrrell Winston mirrors the basketball art found in other flagships.вҒ

posts.caption:
There are people who wear time, and then there are people who define it. @palaktiwarii belongs to the second kind.вңЁ
She carries something rare, a weight that isnвҖҷt borrowed from the past, but isnвҖҷt chasing the future either. It simply exists, with the kind of quiet confidence that doesnвҖҷt need to announce itself.
In this frame, legacy and the now collapse into each other. @guesswatches on her wrist, intent in her eyes, highlighting the вҖңMain CharacterвҖқ hunger behind the gold hardware; this is a story of a woman who isnвҖҷt just wearing the accessories of success, but is actively forging them. вҢҡпёҸ
The Architecture of Time isnвҖҷt a concept. ItвҖҷs a posture. A way of owning every second without letting a single one own you.
@palaktiwarii doesnвҖҷt follow the tempo. She sets it. And right now, sheвҖҷs setting it for all of us. рҹ«¶рҹҸ»

But off-screen things are less grim: Thurman is energized by her work and looking forward to the possibilities of her next chapterвҖ”one where sheвҖҷs making space for new roles, new interests, and more time for herself: вҖңI think the choice to be happy is one that should be made regardless of any conditions.вҖқ

posts.caption:
Introducing our latest cover star, @katehudson!
ItвҖҷs been over 25 years since Kate Hudson earned her first Oscar nomination for her breakout role as Penny Lane in вҖҳAlmost Famous,вҖҷ and sheвҖҷs been shaping the cultural moment ever since. Despite her successвҖ”and her roots in Hollywood royaltyвҖ”sheвҖҷs remained remarkably grounded, continuing to speak about the industry with a genuine sense of gratitude.
вҖңI just really want to make movies that people love,вҖқ Hudson tells LвҖҷOFFICIEL. вҖңItвҖҷs really simple for me. I feel a responsibility as someone that loves this business, and grew up in it, and really wanted to make movies. I hope that I can make movies and shows that people really want to see.вҖқ
That, of course, has always been HudsonвҖҷs strength. Fresh off another Oscar nomination for her role in вҖҳSong Sung Blue,вҖҷ sheвҖҷs now turning her attention back to television with Season 2 of вҖҳRunning Point.вҖҷ In addition to executive producing the Netflix series, Hudson stars as Isla Gordon, a character loosely inspired by Los Angeles Lakers owner and president Jeanie Buss. While the role has prompted comparisons to HudsonвҖҷs own upbringing, sheвҖҷs quick to dismiss that narrative. вҖңJeanie has had to maintain the most important team in sports history, and on top of that, the money and the franchise and everything that goes with it,вҖқ she says. вҖңFor me, weвҖҷre just actors. People are either gonna like what we do or theyвҖҷre not.вҖқ
Read our full interview with Hudson about the collaborative nature of film, her creative process, and growing up in the industry at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
@KateHudson has taken on a wide range of genres, but sheвҖҷs still focused on pushing herself even further. When we asked Hudson what she still hopes to achieve in her career, she didnвҖҷt point to accoladesвҖ”just a desire to keep pursuing roles that scare her, the same approach she brings to everything she does. вҒ 
вҒ 
вҖңI enter every experience like itвҖҷs new, like I donвҖҷt know how itвҖҷs going to unfold,вҖқ she explains. вҖңI come in doing my part, which is understanding what makes me feel confident in the role and the character, but I do that so that when IвҖҷm there, I can be flexible, not rigid.вҖқ Read HudsonвҖҷs full interview at the link in our bio. вҒ 
вҒ

posts.caption:
ItвҖҷs still a very Puccified summer, according to @BellaHadid. Last summer, it felt like everyone вҖ” and their mothers вҖ” were wearing #Pucci, from bikinis to dresses, pants, and everything in between. Once synonymous with 1960s Italian jet-set glamour, PucciвҖҷs signature prints have officially stood the test of time. The recent buzz points to a full-on Pucci-girl summer, marking a broader shift away from understated neutrals and toward maximalist dressing.вҒ 
вҒ 
But where did Pucci begin? Founder @EmilioPucci first gained recognition after designing the first one-piece ski suit before expanding into swimwear and building what would become a global fashion house. His first boutique opened on the island of Capri, and ever since, the brandвҖҷs collections have evoked long days along the romantic Italian coast.вҒ 
вҒ 
At the link in our bio, we unpack how Pucci has stood the test of time вҖ” and how weвҖҷre wearing Pucci prints this summer.вҒ 
вҒ 
Photos courtesy of Pucci and Getty ImagesвҒ 
вҒ 
#PucciPrint #FashionHistory #BellaHadid #EmilioPucci вҒ #HaileybieberвҒ

posts.caption:
вҖңCristГіbal [Balenciaga] has been one of my heroes, first as a source of inspiration and also as someone who always followed his own personal path without looking around,вҖқ Pierpaolo Piccioli told LвҖҷOFFICIEL.вҒ 
вҒ 
Over the last year, Piccioli has stepped into the world of @Balenciaga with a quiet confidence, reshaping its codes while staying rooted in its legacy. For our May Issue, the creative director reflects on his approach to the vast Balenciaga archives.вҒ 
вҒ 
@pppiccioli traveled to Getaria, SpainвҖ”BalenciagaвҖҷs hometownвҖ”to get closer to CristГіbalвҖҷs world, his perspective, and the point of view that shaped it, and to understand what might have pushed him to leave it behind for Paris, a narrative Piccioli finds especially resonant, having been born in Nettuno. вҖңIn the archives, what interested me most wasnвҖҷt the garments themselvesвҖ”which I already knewвҖ”but the experience of wearing them.вҖқвҒ 
вҒ 
Piccioli also reflects on BalenciagaвҖҷs genius in keeping the body at the center of the conversation: вҖңSo he introduced a third element into his aesthetic projectвҖ”airвҖ”between the body and the fabric, creating a new form.вҖқ Tap the link in our bio for the full conversation with the designer.
#balenciaga #pierpaolopiccioli #cristobalbalenciaga

posts.caption:
Have you been counting down the days until spring? So have we. After a particularly unforgiving winter, it feels only right to lean into the promise of warmer daysвҖ”and with it, a return to dressing that feels a little more expressive.вҒ 
вҒ 
The Spring/Summer 2026 runways made one thing clear: neutrals are taking a back seat. Prints are back in full force, from abstract, almost painterly takes at @Ferragamo that blur the line between pattern and modern art, to the effervescent florals at @ChloГ©, and sharp, directional stripes at @Tods.
вҒ 
Here, the print trends weвҖҷre reaching for first from the Spring/Summer 2026 runways.

posts.caption:
Jacob & Co. continues to blur the line between watchmaking, sculpture, and fantasy with the Astronomia вҖңGates of Olympus.вҖқ
Priced at $1.4 million, the one-of-one creation draws inspiration from Greek mythology, featuring Zeus, Olympus, celestial movement, and a fully mechanical miniature world built directly into the timepiece itself.
Inside the 18K rose gold case sits a triple-axis tourbillon, over 9 carats of baguette diamonds, and an intricate Olympus scene designed as part of the movement.
More than a watch, the piece reflects how modern haute horology is increasingly moving toward collectible art and immersive storytelling.
@JacobAndCo #JacobAndCo #Luxurious #Collab

posts.caption:
@Balenciaga dropped its Spring Summer 2026 preview collection in Mumbai last night, and the energy in the room felt different.
With Fatima Sana Sheikh, Bobby Deol, Mouni Roy, Sanya Malhotra, Malavika Mohanan, and Krithi Shetty in attendance, it felt less like a formal showcase and more like a real moment around fashion. The room was sharp, observant, and engaged.
Fatima Sana Shaikh said it best: вҖңBalenciaga always has a chic approach and the brand is now heading towards it even more aggressively.вҖқ And that tone carried through the evening. Nothing felt loud for attention, yet everything commanded it.
What stood out was how people responded. There was curiosity, closer inspection, and genuine interest in the details. Some silhouettes felt familiar but more refined, while others brought a quiet sense of drama without trying too hard.
That is when a collection works.
Not when it demands attention, but when it earns it.
@balenciaga #Balenciaga

posts.caption:
DrakeвҖҷs вҖңICEMANвҖқ rollout has turned into one of the most cinematic automotive showcases ever seen on social media.
Instead of simply flexing a celebrity garage, the series blends hypercars, classics, luxury SUVs, trucks, and even police cars into a dark, film-like visual universe inspired by TorontoвҖҷs cold streets and gritty atmosphere. The lineup includes icons like the Ferrari LaFerrari, F40, F50, Daytona SP3, Monza SP2, McLaren Elva, Aston Martin Valhalla, Rolls-Royce Spectre, Hummer H1, GMC Typhoon, and Chevrolet C10.
What made the rollout stand out wasnвҖҷt just the value of the cars, but the storytelling itself. Rain-soaked streets, cinematic lighting, slow-motion sequences, and moody visuals transformed each vehicle into part of a larger aesthetic tied directly to DrakeвҖҷs personality and creative direction.
Rather than feeling like traditional luxury content, вҖңICEMANвҖқ felt closer to a high-end automotive film series built around culture, music, and design.
Images: @champagnepapi & Courtesy of Brands
posts.alt_text: Photo by Luxurious on May 26, 2026. May be an image of car, magazine and text that says 'CARS Drake just showed off $29 million worth of cars in the ICEMAN album LUXURIOUS MAP MAR'.

posts.caption:
Ducati has entered the luxury coffee space with a track-inspired espresso machine built like a superbike.
Created to celebrate the brandвҖҷs 100th anniversary, the limited-edition Ducati Barista M3 1926 features carbon-fibre panels inspired by Ducati race bikes, pressure gauges styled like motorcycle instrumentation, and a design language pulled directly from the brandвҖҷs performance heritage. Only 1,926 units will be produced globally as a tribute to DucatiвҖҷs founding year.
The machine reflects the growing crossover between automotive culture, luxury design, and lifestyle products, where brands are increasingly extending beyond vehicles into collectible experiences and home luxury. Online, enthusiasts have already called it one of DucatiвҖҷs most unexpected collaborations yet.
Images: @ducati

posts.caption:
SK-II is making a major push into India as the countryвҖҷs luxury skincare market continues to grow rapidly.
The Japanese beauty giant recently entered India with its iconic PITERA-powered Facial Treatment Essence, betting on a new generation of Indian consumers increasingly investing in premium, science-led skincare rather than traditional beauty routines. Industry analysts now view India as one of the worldвҖҷs fastest-growing luxury beauty markets, driven by younger consumers, rising disposable incomes, and growing awareness around long-term skin health.
To strengthen its India expansion, SK-II has partnered with Nykaa and Galeries Lafayette while introducing AI-powered skin analysis tools, immersive retail experiences, and personalised skincare consultations across key cities including Mumbai, Delhi, Bengaluru, and Hyderabad.
The move also reflects a wider shift happening across the global beauty industry, where luxury skincare brands are increasingly viewing India as the next major growth market alongside China and the Middle East.

posts.caption:
#ManifestCover
In ManifestвҖҷs May-June cover interview, Rukmini Vasanth (@rukmini_vasanth) reflects on grief, love, and the kind of resilience that quietly reshapes a person over time. Beneath the poise is a life shaped by movement, loss, and emotional inheritance, experiences that continue to inform the way she understands connection and presence.
вҖңYou donвҖҷt know what life will throw at you. But as long as youвҖҷre there for each other, thatвҖҷs what matters. ItвҖҷs about support and presence more than anything else,вҖқ she shares.

posts.caption:
#ManifestCover
There is a quiet power to Rukmini Vasanth (@rukmini_vasanth). One that doesnвҖҷt seek attention, yet holds it effortlessly.
For ManifestвҖҷs MayвҖ“June cover, she steps into an imaginative world where temple jewellery meets contemporary silhouettes, blurring the lines between memory and modernity. She embodies a woman in transition, grounded, luminous, and yet constantly becoming.
вҖңIвҖҷm not very good at describing myself,вҖқ she says, almost in passing.
Yet, in the same breath, her choices reveal a quiet clarity of intent. вҖңI donвҖҷt want to isolate my character. I need to understand the whole world it exists in.вҖқ
Between restraint and reinvention, stillness and movement, she exists in that rare in-between. Not fully defined, not confined. Just on the cusp of a wider sky.

Ceremonial Bags That ArenвҖҷt Potlis!
There is something about the good old potli bag that echos timlessness. The bag was relevant ten years ago, is relevant today, and chances are, that weвҖҷll see them everywhere during the wedding season ten years from now as well.
However, confession: while we love a good old potli bag, sometimes, we want to ditch them for minaudiГЁres or wristlets, or even Y2K bags! If you too, would like to get a break from the potli this wedding season, here are the bags to get instead!

posts.caption:
Miles Davis, the jazz legend and style innovator who would have turned 100 on May 26, remains for many people the pre-eminent avatar of cool. And while DavisвҖҷs greatest legacy is musical, he also cut a distinctive image over the course of his five-decade career. (Davis died of pneumonia in 1991, at 65.) His style shifted alongside his sound, but he had his touchstones вҖ” face-obscuring sunglasses and ticket-magnet sports cars among them.
For DavisвҖҷs centennial, our writers reflected on the specific elements of the trumpeterвҖҷs public image that made him a genius of cool. From his fast cars to his huge shades, learn about all the things that made Miles Miles at the link in our bio. Photos by Michael Ochs Archives/Getty Images, David Redfern/Redferns, Getty, Christian Rose/Roger Viollet, via Getty Images, Mark Patiky/CondГ© Nast, via Getty Images

Some very powerful professional women do not use them, preferring clothing with pockets and/or brief cases. Is the age of the handbag over?"
Fashion may be at a pivot point when it comes to handbags. At the link in our bio, @vvfriedman explains the trend. Photo by Gotham/GC Images

posts.caption:
As booksellers try to figure out how to market audiobooks in person, Audible, the audiobook giant owned by Amazon, staged a pop-up bookstore вҖ” without any physical books.
The Audible Story House, a three-story emporium on the Lower East Side of Manhattan, has an event space for craft nights and author panels and a cafe selling matcha lattes and branded merch. The shop also has two large listening lounges equipped with surround-sound, listening booths where individuals or small groups can sample the audiobooks and a listening bar where patrons can consult a вҖңstory tenderвҖқ to get personalized recommendations from a list of titles available on Audible.
вҖңPeople want not only to have IRL experiences, they want things that are tangible and not just digital,вҖқ said James Finn, AudibleвҖҷs global head of brand and content marketing.
Some independent booksellers have shifted their sales strategies in the hopes of winning over the audiobook consumer вҖ” and staying competitive against giants like Audible. Are bookless bookstores a solution? Tap the link in our bio to read the full story. Photos by @sethdcaplan
posts.alt_text: Photo by New York Times Fashion & Style on May 23, 2026. May be an image of book and text.

posts.caption:
While the Cannes Film Festival red carpets and events were lighter than usual on Hollywood A-listers, they made up for it in memorable fashion moments.
Over the course of the festival each May, stars celebrate films that are sure to populate next yearвҖҷs Oscar ballot. And they dress the part.
The festival, with its innumerable photo calls, hotel sightings, panels, parties and premieres, allows us to see the range in a celebrityвҖҷs style, with many serving up multiple looks a day. Of all the fashion that descended on the French Riviera over the past week and a half, 15 ensembles stood out. Tap the link in our bio to see all the unforgettable looks from Cannes. Photos by Manon Cruz/Reuters, John Locher/Invision, via @apnews, Andreas Rentz/Getty Images, Thibaud Moritz/Agence France-Presse вҖ” Getty Images, Hoda Davaine/Getty Images, Scott A Garfitt/Invision, via @apnews

posts.caption:
Stephen ColbertвҖҷs 11-season run behind the вҖңLate ShowвҖқ desk came to a close last night. And with that, there is one less man in a suit to watch on late-night television.
For the better part of a half-century, the suited late-night host has been one of TVвҖҷs stubborn constants. And Colbert hewed to the uniform: dark suit, pressed white shirt and unremarkable tie.
"His jackets were always single-breasted, and most of them were dark blue, with some gray peppered in. A recent charity auction revealed that his wardrobe included suits from Zegna and Giorgio Armani. Some were faintly striped or checked, but still, there was not much raffish about them," our fashion reporter @jacobwgallagher wrote.
A totem of conventionality, the suit has become a useful tool for many late-night hosts. But the look was not always so uniform. At the link in our bio, read more about how the suited late-night host became convention вҖ” and how Colbert dressed the part. Photo by Scott Kowalchyk/CBS
posts.alt_text: Photo by New York Times Fashion & Style on May 22, 2026. May be an image of one or more people and text that says 'Why Stephen Colbert's Suits Were a Useful Tool'.

posts.caption:
Shein, the online retailer that has become the de facto face of ultra fast fashion, has acquired Everlane, a U.S. retailer branded as sustainable, according to EverlaneвҖҷs chief executive.
In a deal that was finalized on Friday, L Catterton, the private equity firm backed by luxury conglomerate LVMH, sold its majority stake in Everlane. The company would not say how much it was sold for, but Puck News had reported over the weekend that the price was $100 million. The deal set off a wave of backlash for Everlane, whose consumers saw the proposed sale to the ultra fast fashion giant as a betrayal of the brandвҖҷs climate-conscious ethos.
Alfred Chang, EverlaneвҖҷs chief executive, said in a statement to The New York Times that Everlane will вҖңremain an independent brandвҖқ and keep its вҖңsustainability commitments.вҖқ He framed the acquisition as a way to expand the brandвҖҷs global reach and вҖңaccelerateвҖқ their vision. Chang will remain in his role, according to a memo sent to staff by Chang and EverlaneвҖҷs leadership and seen by The Times.
Read more details about the acquisition at the link in our bio. Photo by @justinkaneps
posts.alt_text: Photo by New York Times Fashion & Style on May 22, 2026. May be an image of text that says 'Everlane Finalizes Sale to to Fast Fashion Giant Shein "бһңбһ“'.

posts.caption:
WeвҖҷve seen #JonathanAnderson continue to use references that carry emotional memory, whether thatвҖҷs art, ceramics, craftвҖҰ and now childrenвҖҷs illustrations!
His latest #Dior collection (first teased in November 25) features #TheVeryHungryCaterpillar by Eric Carle. ItвҖҷs one of the most universally recognisable childrenвҖҷs books, instantly familiar across generations. For many including myself, it was one of the first books I encountered as a child. ItвҖҷs fun to see Jonathan Anderson continue to mix DiorвҖҷs couture sensibilities with playful and whimsical references. The result feels nostalgic, ironic, emotional, and unserious, elevating childhood memory into collectible fashion.

When we think of archival fashion, the conversation is often dominated by the great Western maisons, but seeing #BellaHadid recently wear archival pieces from #ElieSaab, #ZuhairMurad, and #GeorgesChakra feels like a long-overdue reminder of how influential the work coming out of Lebanon was, and still is.
Lebanese designers really helped define the visual language of 2000s glamour at a time when much of fashion was moving toward minimalism. Their #HauteCouture embodied extravagance rooted in skills, passed down over generations across the Middle East.
(Images courtesy of Rani Fawaz/ Yasmine Diba / Bella Hadid / Elie Saab / Diggzy / Zuhair Murad / Getty / Backgrid )

posts.caption:
@balenciagaвҖҷs new #ManoloBlahnik collaboration makes perfect sense for #PierpaoloPiccioliвҖҷs evolving vision for the house. It shows where #Balenciaga is headed after years defined by irony, provocation, and streetwear. Signalling to a sense of couture and refinement, we havenвҖҷt really seen in a while.
By collaborating with one of the most revered shoe designers, I love how Piccioli references the shared Spanish heritage of #CristГіbalBalenciaga and @manoloblahnik which makes the partnership feel rooted in something deeper than celeb hype or marketing.
While theres still exaggerated sneakers for the fanbase established under #Demna, a pair of hand made crystal satin slingbacks seems to be the perfect welcome back for the customer searching for glamour and femininity once again.
What do you think of the collab?

posts.caption:
Angels do exist, and they wear @i.migmoon, the 21-year-old Russian and South Korean student whose dresses i cant stop thinking about.  Inspired by his parents, who were ballet dancers, Ilya spent much of his childhood attending rehearsals, where he watched ballerinas warming up and practicing in delicate feathered tutus.
That early exposure to the world of ballet continues to shape his work today. He loves using wings and feathers explaining how he wants to elevate women into divine heroines like an angel or superhero. #Couture #IlyaMigmoon

posts.caption:
#CallForSubmissions | Memory can comfort us, connect us, haunt us, or change us over time. It preserves moments we long to hold onto, blurs those we wish to forget, and lingers in unexpected ways.
We invite you to reflect on the nature of memory, its permanence and fragility, and its power to distort or preserve our perception. Write about what you remember, what you lost, and what remains.
YOUR ARTICLE COULD BE PUBLISHED ON WWW.SHETHEPEOPLE.TV
Guidelines:
* 700-800 words.

posts.caption:
At 40, Bengaluru's Ashwini Ganapathi has made history as the first Indian woman to complete the Asia Trail Master (ATM) Grandmaster Quest вҖ” one of the toughest endurance trail-running challenges.
The Quest requires runners to complete six races of 70km or more within TWO YEARS across different countries and terrains.
Ashwini began the challenge in 2024 with races across Malaysia, Japan, and Vietnam, including multiple 100-mile ultras.
Her final race in the two-year challenge came at Merapoh Rainforest Trail in Pahang, Malaysia, on May 1. She navigated a gruelling 164-km course with 3,700 metres of elevation gain through rainforest terrain.
She completed the run in 28 hours and 33 minutes, finishing 5th overall among 146 starters and 2nd among 27 women participants.
Source: The Times of India
Visit www.shethepeople.tv for more.

The Indo-Tibetan Border Police (ITBP) achieved a historic milestone as its first-ever all-women mountaineering expedition successfully summited Mount Everest.
All 11 women climbers reached the summit of the worldвҖҷs highest peak, standing at 8,848 metres, via the South Col Route. The first successful summit was recorded on May 21 at 6:52 am.
The South Col Route, one of the most widelyused yet physically demanding approaches to Everest, involves navigating harsh weather conditions, steep ice walls, and oxygen-deficient high-altitude zones.

posts.caption:
Some combinations have a way of making a look feel instantly, undeniably luxuriousвҖҰ quietly, yet completely. вңЁ
Think of your wedding outfit colour not just as a shade you loveвҖҰ but as the mood you walk into the room with.
The feeling that settles before anyone sees the embroidery, the jewellery, or the detail work.
The red and antique gold that carries centuries of bridal legacy and still somehow feels like it was made for right now. The ivory and gold that whispers old money, quiet confidence, and a kind of elegance that doesnвҖҷt need to announce itself. The powder blue and grey that feels like the inside of a dream - cool, ethereal, and impossibly photogenic. The lilac with soft silver that makes every photograph look like it was taken in a garden at golden hour. рҹ’«
ItвҖҷs never just about the colourвҖҰ ItвҖҷs about the feeling of wearing that particular combination and knowing - completely, without any doubt - that this is the one.
Because the most luxurious bridal looks arenвҖҷt always built on the most expensive fabricsвҖҰ TheyвҖҷre the ones where the colour, the light, and the woman wearing it simply fall perfectly into place.
In 2026, bridal colour palettes are moving far beyond conventional choices - from emerald and beige pairings that feel richly modern, to soft peach and ivory tones that photograph like a fairytale, to monochrome beige that redefines what bridal luxury actually looks like.
So if your bridal outfit colour could set the entire mood of your weddingвҖҰ which combination would you choose? рҹӨҚ

There are moments in a wedding that no amount of rehearsal can prepare you for.
And then there is this - petals falling like the universe decided to participate, a bride in deep red looking up with a smile she couldnвҖҷt contain even if she tried, and a groom reaching for her with the kind of certainty that makes everyone watching exhale at once.
The jaimala, as it was always meant to be felt.
And then there was them. вқӨпёҸ
But what made Shruti and RohanвҖҷs ceremony linger long after the last frame was the feeling that ran through all of it - the lush greens framing the moment, the warmth of an outdoor celebration in full bloom, and two people standing in the middle of it all looking like they couldnвҖҷt quite believe their luck. The good kind of disbelief.
Some weddings are beautiful to look at.
And some stay with you because of what you feel when you look - the petals mid-air, the smile breaking through, and a love story arriving exactly on time. рҹҢҹ

And then there are ceremonies that are felt - where the rituals carry their full weight, where the laughter that breaks through isnвҖҷt a distraction but the whole point, and where the camera disappears so completely that what remains is just the truth of two people stepping into something together.
This was that ceremony.
And then there was them. вқӨпёҸ
But what made this more than just a beautiful wedding was how unguarded it all felt - the Talambralu unfolding with the kind of warmth that no amount of staging could manufacture, the glances exchanged when nobody was supposed to be looking, the affection that moved through the frame like it had always lived there.
Some weddings are produced.
And some stay with you because they were simply, honestly lived - every smile earned, every moment real, and a love story told with the kind of ease that only comes when itвҖҷs genuine. рҹҢҹ
