posts.caption:
Fashion styling is a job. Not a hobby. Not a passion project. Not something people do on the side because they love clothes.
It is a profession that takes years to build. The eye. The contacts. The knowledge of how the industry actually works across every sector, every brief, every type of client. That does not come from loving fashion. It comes from years of showing up, doing the work and figuring it out mostly alone.
And yet it is still one of the most undervalued roles in the creative industry.
Stylists are asked to work for free. For credit. For exposure. For a rate that does not come close to reflecting the skill, the hours or the experience involved. And somewhere along the way the industry decided that was normal. That stylists should be grateful for the opportunity. That loving what you do means you should not expect to be paid properly for it.
We are past that.
Behind every iconic image, every red carpet moment, every campaign that stopped you mid-scroll, there was a stylist. Building the looks, interpreting the brief, managing the pressure, absorbing the chaos so nobody else had to. Often without a credit. Sometimes without a proper rate.
That changes when stylists know their worth, charge it without apology and stop accepting the jobs that treat their career like a favour.
вһЎпёҸвһЎпёҸ If you are a working fashion stylist who is ready to do exactly that, comment STUDIO and we will send you the free trial link to The Studio of Stylist Elixir. Rate guides. Contracts. Portfolio tools. Masterclasses every Monday. Everything you need to build a career that reflects what you are actually worth.

posts.caption:
The right clients do not just pay better. They change the entire shape of your career.
Every client you say yes to tells the industry what kind of fashion stylist you are. Take the work that drains you and underpays you, and that becomes the career you are known for. So the goal was never more clients. It was the right ones. The ones who pay properly, respect your creative direction, and book you again. A handful of those is worth more than a calendar full of the wrong ones.
Here is the part nobody likes to hear. Dream clients do not find you by accident. They find you when your portfolio shows the work you want more of, your rates signal your level, and your positioning makes it obvious who you are for. That is built on purpose, not luck.
That is exactly what the six weeks are for. The Fashion Mentorship Program rebuilds your portfolio, your rates, and your positioning, so the clients who actually serve your career are the ones coming to you.
вңҰ You do not need more clients. You need the right ones. вңҰ
You leave with a portfolio that books, rates you can hold, and positioning that attracts the right clients and filters out the wrong ones. Weekly group sessions and private 1:1 calls the whole way through.

posts.caption:
Growing a fashion styling business is the part almost nobody talks about, because styling alone has a ceiling.
There are only so many days in the week and so much one person can charge. Real growth is not a higher day rate. It is moving from stylist to creative director, building brand partnerships instead of one-off bookings, bringing in a team that works under your name, reading where the industry is heading before it shifts, and never relying on one income stream. And at the very top, building a business with a name and a value beyond you, that could one day run, or even sell, without you in the room.
вңҰ Getting booked is one skill. Growing past your own two hands is another entirely. вңҰ
This is the final part of a series on building a fashion styling career, from breaking in, to going solo, to growing the business.

posts.caption:
The leap from fashion assistant to fashion stylist is the one nobody feels ready for.
You will never feel fully ready, so you make the leap before you do. You take your own jobs while still assisting, until your work can carry you. Your book shifts from their vision to yours. You learn to charge like a stylist, not an assistant. The client becomes yours, and so does the risk. Your network becomes your lifeline. And the moment you go solo, you are running a business, not just a craft.
It is frightening. It is uncertain. And it is the best decision most fashion stylists ever make.
вңҰ The career you want does not start until you back yourself enough to step out on your own. вңҰ
This is part two of a series on building a fashion styling career, from breaking in, to going solo, to growing the business.

posts.caption:
Breaking into fashion styling is the part nobody hands you a map for.
Start by assisting, because the set teaches what no course can. Learn the process, the call-ins, returns, and fittings that are the actual job. Learn the history, because that is where references come from. Learn what is happening now, because taste is current and historic at the same time. Find your area. Build a book with a point of view. And remember the whole career is relationships.
It is a lot. That is exactly the point.
вңҰ Nobody figures it out alone, and nobody hands you the map. вңҰ

posts.caption:
The male body, once allowed a certain laziness, is now expected to show its working. At some point, getting hotter stopped sounding fun.
Looksmaxxing sits at that extreme end. It is male vanity after it has been given a scoring system. In a culture where status, masculinity and identity can feel unstable, looksmaxxing offers the illusion of control.
The manosphere gives that promise its sharper ideological edge, folding self-improvement into fear of rejection, romantic inadequacy, peer comparison and the belief that appearance determines social worth.
Terms like вҖңlow-tier manвҖқ, вҖңsubhumanвҖқ and вҖңGiga ChadвҖқ reduce human identity into rankings and visual metrics.
вҖңBone-smashing is dangerous and has no scientific basis whatsoever. Mewing is wildly overhyped; there is no robust clinical evidence it meaningfully reshapes adult craniofacial structure,вҖқ says Dr Macrene Alexiades.
Head to the link in bio to read why the future of looksmaxxing may not be its most brutal version, but its most ordinary-looking one.
By Ria Bhatia (@riabbhatia)

posts.caption:
Collagen powder has slipped from supplement jars into smoothie bowls and beauty cabinets, sold as shortcuts to glow. But hype does not equal efficacy.
Experts point out that collagen does not rebuild skin. It provides building blocks and signals that can support collagen production.
Marine, bovine, and vegan collagen each come with trade-offs, but transparency and consistency matter more than marketing. Visible results require eight to twelve weeks of disciplined use.

posts.caption: In most animal species, nature tends to maintain a roughly equal number of males and females because this balance maximizes reproductive success over generations. However, in many situations, populations may become female-biased because females are more important for increasing population size; a single male can reproduce with multiple females, but each female can produce only a limited number of offspring. In addition, males in many species face higher mortality due to fighting, competition, risk-taking, and greater visibility to predators. Some animals, such as bees and ants, naturally produce overwhelmingly female populations because only a few males are needed for reproduction. Thus, evolution does not intentionally вҖңfavorвҖқ females, but biological and environmental pressures often make female-heavy populations more advantageous for survival and recovery.

posts.caption:
There is a point where jewellery stops behaving like ornament and starts asking to be read. New York-based label Eskae (@eskae.atelier) sits in that space, creating sculptural pieces that challenge the polite expectations of modern jewellery. вҖңI was inspired to start ESKAE by a desire to create jewellery that holds meaning beyond adornmentвҖҰ ESKAE is a play on the suffix вҖҳesqueвҖҷ, suggesting the essence of something rather than a fixed definition. I wanted to build a brand that feels like a state of being rather than a label, something that invites interpretation,вҖқ says founder Drishti Verma (@drishtivermaa).
Born in India and now based in New York City, Verma works between two cultural registers. IndiaвҖҷs layered traditions and New YorkвҖҷs global landscape have shaped her understanding of вҖңform, meaning and universality and how intimate belief systems can be shared and understood across different contexts.вҖқ In her work, that duality appears through contrast: structure and fluidity, restraint and expression, silence and intensity.
Drawing from mythology, philosophy and psychology, Eskae is anchored in what Verma calls the вҖңunspokenвҖқ: emotional states that exist before language. The result is jewellery that feels sculptural, intimate and alive.

posts.caption:
By the way, the wristwatch was invented by women. For centuries, wristwatches were actually considered jewelryвҖ”delicate, decorative, and almost exclusively feminineвҖ”while men relied on pocket watches for вҖңseriousвҖқ timekeeping. The earliest recorded wristwatch commission dates back to 1810, when Caroline Murat, NapoleonвҖҷs younger sister, ordered a bracelet watch from Breguet.
So yes, while male watchmakers engineered the mechanics, women were the ones who transformed the wristwatch into a wearable fashion category in the first place.
Ironically, men didnвҖҷt fully embrace wristwatches until military conflicts in the late 19th and early 20th centuries made pocket watches impractical.
Fashion history stays humbling people daily.
#WatchHistory #FashionHistory #Breguet #LuxuryWatches #TheCitizensPoste
posts.alt_text: Photo by The CitizenвҖҷs Poste on May 25, 2026. May be an image of wrist watch, drink and text that says 'By The Way, the Wristwatch Was Invented by Women'.

posts.caption:
ItвҖҷs Simon Porte JacquemusвҖҷ world and weвҖҷre honestly just living in it. What makes Jacquemus so dominant right now is that the brand doesnвҖҷt just sell clothes, it sells feeling: Mediterranean fantasy, exaggerated romance, humor, intimacy, sunshine, nostalgia. Everything feels emotional before it feels commercial.
And somehow, Jacquemus has managed to turn that emotion into one of the smartest luxury marketing machines in fashion. Viral runway moments, giant surrealist installations, hyper-shareable campaigns, celebrity casting, raw personal storytellingвҖ”all while still maintaining the feeling of an independent dream rather than a corporate luxury machine.
That balance is incredibly hard to fake.
Fashion people love to talk about вҖңworld-building,вҖқ but Jacquemus is one of the few brands actually doing it successfully.
#Jacquemus #SimonPorteJacquemus #FashionMarketing #LuxuryFashion #TheCitizensPoste
posts.alt_text: Photo by The CitizenвҖҷs Poste on May 24, 2026. May be an image of clothes hanger, umbrella, curtains and text that says 'It's Jacquemus' World, and We're Just Living in It'.

posts.caption:
This human artist creates oil paintings that literally look like dreams and I need one in my life immediatelyвҖ”and no, theyвҖҷre not AI.
@diango.hernandez is a Cuban artist and designer whose work constantly gets mistaken for digital art online because the paintings are that surreal, but itвҖҷs all done by hand. HernГЎndez blends his background in industrial design with traditional oil painting techniques, creating these hyper-precise compositions that still somehow feel emotional and unstable at the same time.
Honestly one of those artists that makes you stare at a painting for ten minutes trying to figure out how anyone could think human art could ever be replaced.
Literally insane.
#ContemporaryArt #DiangoHernandez #OilPainting #ArtWorld #TheCitizensPoste
posts.alt_text: Photo by The CitizenвҖҷs Poste on May 23, 2026. May be an image of poster and text that says 'This Human Artist Is Completely Outplaying AI Right Now'.

posts.caption:
If any other fashion people out there are starting to fall for the art world, just know youвҖҷre not alone. Fashion and fine art naturally pull toward each other because theyвҖҷre both visual languages rooted in emotion, identity, and cultural commentary. And honestly, after enough trend cycles and market reports, it makes sense that so many people in fashion start craving something a little slower, more permanent, and less commercially reactive.
A lot of the industryвҖҷs most interesting creatives eventually drift toward art because it offers a different kind of freedom вҖ” one less tied to seasons, algorithms, and wearability. The overlap has always existed, but right now it feels especially strong.
Maybe weвҖҷre all just looking for substance again (IвҖҷm projecting).
#Fashion #ArtWorld #ContemporaryArt #FashionCulture #TheCitizensPoste

posts.caption:
@realbarbarapalvin delivered one of the standout maternity style moments of the 2026 Cannes Film Festival, announcing her first pregnancy with @dylansprouse through a series of major red-carpet looks styled by @marc_eram.
From a custom powder-blue Miu Miu chiffon gown at Parallel Tales to a sleek вҖҳ90s-inspired Karl Lagerfeld tuxedo shirt and dramatic black skirt for Karma, Palvin balanced softness with sharp tailoring throughout the week. She also embraced bold silhouettes in an oversized pink gown by Willy Chavarria and an ethereal off-the-shoulder Ella Mae look for Paper Tiger.
What really made the Cannes run memorable, though, was the energy between the couple. While Dylan kept things classic in black tuxedos, the two broke from the usual red carpet stiffness by laughing with photographers and leaning fully into the moment.
We love our girl.
#BarbaraPalvin #DylanSprouse #CannesFilmFestival #MaternityStyle #RedCarpetFashion

posts.caption:
This week, fashion felt like it was rewriting its own playbook in real time. On one side: spectacle. While Cannes Film Festival is taking over France, Demna turned Times Square into a chaotic вҖңGucciCoreвҖқ runway for Gucci, and Matthieu BlazyвҖҷs vision at Chanel already appears to be paying off commercially, helping push the house back to the center of the luxury conversation.
On the other: vulnerability and personal narrative. Anok Yai returned to the cover of British Vogue alongside her parents after a serious health battle, Rosie Huntington-Whiteley leaned further into quiet luxury through her heirloom-focused MГ©moire project, and Bad Bunny stripped things back for a deeply personal Zara collaboration rooted in home and identity.
Caught up? For now.
#FashionNews #LuxuryFashion #Gucci #Chanel #Cannes

posts.caption:
Look at anyoneвҖҷs online bio today, and youвҖҷll find a grocery list of skills and accomplishments. No oneвҖҷs just one thing.
OnвҖҜвҖҳThe NodвҖҷ team masthead itself youвҖҷll see an audience growth manager who runs a bookstagram. Our social media editor at work goes home to make comedic reels. And our fashion writer, weвҖҷre convinced, should have her own YouTube channel. ItвҖҷs as 2026 as it can be.
Of course, our cover girl Samantha Ruth Prabhu (@samantharuthprabhuoffl) represents everything weвҖҷre talking aboutвҖ”movie star, producer, serial entrepreneur, chatty podcaster. A multi-hyphenate in the true sense of the term.
As The Nod turns two, all this month we will feature an impressive array of all-rounders: a brand strategist with an alter ego, a BAFTA-nominated actor dabbling in prog rock, a hustler with more jobs than days in a week, an architect whoвҖҷs a drag queenвҖҰ
But are these side hustlesвҖҜa great work-life blend to save us all from burnout orвҖҜjust faux-inspirational talesвҖҜdesigned asвҖҜcoping mechanism? Is it a way to ensure that weвҖҷre relevant in a chaotic time where job security is dismal, technology is throwing new realities our way, and the world is at war? Or is it our unsatiable curiosity that is leading us there? This May, join us as we explore вҖҳThe Age of theвҖҜPoly-ProfessionalвҖҷ.
posts.alt_text: Photo by The Nod on May 18, 2026. May be a graphic of magazine, poster and text that says 'We're a generation that for long was told that we can do anything, be anything. Finally, we're paying heed. Welcome to the age of universal polymathy, or, as we're calling it, the Age of the Poly-Professional.'.

posts.caption:
For Adarsh Gourav (@gouravadarsh)вҖ”the BAFTA-nominated actor who had the dream debut of playing a younger Shah Rukh Khan in вҖҳMy Name is KhanвҖҷ (2010) and has, in the last 15 years, worked with bucket-list filmmakers like Zoya Akhtar, Bejoy Nambiar, Ramin Bahrani, and Arjun Varain SinghвҖ”music has always been his first love. ItвҖҷs a love seeded in his childhood, one that has stuck despite often being unrequited, through rejection, failure, and harsh realisations. It has been his Everest, and it has given him everything he has today; he would likely not be on screen if it werenвҖҷt for being вҖңnoticedвҖқ while singing вҖҳVande MataramвҖҷ at the Kala Ghoda Arts Festival as a teenager, which really put him on the path to auditioning for films.
вҖңIвҖҷve always wanted to make music. I wanted to be a playback singer,вҖқ he says now, relaying a parallel life story thatвҖҷs stayed under wraps until very recently. вҖңAnd now IвҖҷm just using my street cred as an actor to push this side of myself. And why not? I hope theyвҖҷll think: вҖҳActing achhi kar leta hai toh ye bhi theek thaak kar hi leta hoga.вҖҷвҖқ
At the link in bio, @_niddee_ chats with the polymath about the rare vantage of enjoying the best of both worlds.

posts.caption:
Growing up in ChennaiвҖҜasвҖҜthe youngest of threeвҖҜsiblings, actingвҖҜwasnвҖҷtвҖҜpart of the plan for model student Samantha Ruth Prabhu (@samantharuthprabhuoffl). During her gap year after college, the then 21-year-old dabbled in modelling and ended up at a film audition for kicks. вҖңI thought if I do get it, IвҖҷll do it for a year and then go back to studying,вҖқ she says. вҖңThe idea was to do it temporarily, never make it a career.вҖҜIвҖҷmвҖҜsure my teachers were disappointed when I became an actor. I think some of them still are,вҖқ she laughs.
WeвҖҷre not the first to ask her what her alternative career would be, but this time she has an answer: вҖңIвҖҷm doing it now,вҖқ she says with excitement. вҖңI think this entrepreneurial journey is what IвҖҷve always wanted to do,вҖқ says the serial entrepreneur, whose work straddles films, beauty, wellness, fashion, food, and sport, all at once.
For our second anniversary issue, @chloechou13 flew to Hyderabad to chat with our cover star. Full interview at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
вҖңLast week, I binged-through Imperfect Women, a new psychological thriller on Apple TV(@appletv) starring Kerry Washington(@kerrywashington), Kate Mara(@katemara), Elisabeth Moss and Joel Kinnaman(@joelkinnaman), and based on a 2021 novel of the same by Araminta Hall(@aramintahall). The story is about three friends, Eleanor (Washington), Nancy (Mara) and Mary (Moss) who met in college and have, вҖңa kinship from deep within their souls.вҖқ When their three-decade long friendship is shattered by a murder, the cracks and fissures in their seemingly perfect lives come through.
There are so many talking points that you could dissect hereвҖ”the definition of ambition for women, the complexities of friendship as adults, maintaining a cultivated social image versus living authentically. But as a long-time fashion writer, itвҖҷs the styling that I always keep an eye out for, especially in a show like this thatвҖҷs set in Los Angeles, a city where wealth and glamour are mainstays. And itвҖҷs for the third member of this trioвҖ”a woman from a wealthy family, running a much-respected non-profit, Eleanor BouchetвҖ”where the fashion gets really interesting.вҖқ
At the link in bio, @priyankaskhanna in conversation with costume designer Tiffany Hasbourne about the jewellery in Apple TVвҖҷs propulsive new thriller.

posts.caption:
Cactus plants, date palms, a sprig of bright yellow amaltas flowers or pink ker plants, maybe even a pot of chaiвҖ”these are the motifs that designer Rina Singh(@rinasing09) of the Delhi-based label Eka(@ekaco) stamped onto the looks for her first collaboration with Swadesh(@swadesh_online). вҖңIf you have to sum up a road trip to Rajasthan in six objects, what are those objects? Basically, that was the story,вҖқ says Singh about the collection, which is an extension of her spring/summer 2026 line.
ItвҖҷs the first time that Singh has collaborated with the craft-forward retailer, and in some ways she used it as the opportunity to go back home. вҖңI was thinking about what the woman who would buy this would wear to her hometown. Like, when I go home to Kurukshetra, I wear my whole set with a dupatta, whereas the Eka woman might wear the dupatta on its own or with a dress instead of a kurta... ItвҖҷs up to her,вҖқ she says.
At the link in bio, @butoolbegum on why this collab is the perfect addition to your summer wardrobe.

posts.caption:
For the longest time, AmritsarвҖҷs travel itinerary has followed a pretty predictable script: a stop at the Golden Temple followed by dhaba-hopping for chhole-kulchey, buttery black dal, towering glasses of lassi, and enough ghee to make your cardiologist nervous. The city has always worn its food culture loudly and proudly. But lately, alongside old-school institutions like Kesar Da Dhaba and Brothers Dhaba, Amritsar has also been making room for a different kind of dining. One with cocktail bars, mood-lit restaurants, and people actually making reservations to go out for dinner.
Much of that shift can be credited to Kavish(@kavishkhurana) and Shruti Khurana(@shrutikhuranaa), the couple behind some of the cityвҖҷs most popular new-age dining spots.Their spaces have always gravitated towards old-world drama and nostalgia in their decor. Their newest venture, Governor House(@thegovernorhousebyelgin), located in the same complex as Sarovar Premiere, in the upscale Mohindra Colony, takes that aesthetic to its grandest scale yet.
At the link in bio, read @geetikasachdevвҖҷs full review of the restaurant.

posts.caption:
You are absolutely and completely wrong.
Heritage survives only when people actually WANT to wear it. Which means it has to become fashion first. That is how textile cultures survive вҖ” through reinvention, adaptation, and changing with people.
Also, вҖңrestraintвҖқ?
Which part of Indian textiles suggests restraint exactly?
The colours?
The zari?
The brocades?
The regional drapes?
The fact that sari drapes changed across states depending on labour, climate, movement, and lifestyle?
Indian textiles were never static museum objects. They evolved constantly because people changed them constantly.
If nobody reinvented craft, adapted silhouettes, changed drapes, experimented with colour, or made textiles fashionable for newer generations, most Indian crafts would have died under mill-made polyester decades ago.
Invention is not the corruption of heritage.
It is the reason heritage survives.
Please do some actual research before posting something this historically unaware about Indian textiles, their evolution, their function, and their place in contemporary life.

posts.caption:
вҖңCannes can start now!вҖқ declared actor Eva Longoria as she joined fellow LвҖҷOrГ©al Paris ambassador Aishwarya Rai Bachchan on stage at the cosmetic giantвҖҷs photo-op at the 79th Cannes Film Festival on Friday. And for many die-hard red carpet watchers, Cannes truly did begin this weekend as IndiaвҖҷs uncrowned вҖҳQueen of CannesвҖҷ stepped onto the Croisette once again. Rai Bachchan, arguably one of the festivalвҖҷs most watched, debated and dissected celebrities, had already sent expectations soaring after news broke that stylist and designer Mohit Rai would helm her wardrobe this year.
For her first appearance, ARB arrived in an вҖҳabyss blueвҖҷ custom sculptural couture gown by Amit Aggarwal featuring his signature crystal vein embroidery. Day two saw her in a draped bodice gown by Sophie Couture complete with a cape-like dupatta detail. Were the outfits groundbreaking couture? No. The silhouettes, styling and dupatta flourishes werenвҖҷt too far removed from her usual wheelhouse. Did she look lovely? Absolutely. The colours worked beautifully on her and the beauty choices were refreshingly restrained (goodbye, purple lipstick). Were these looks for the books? Perhaps not.
But for the Bollywood star who has walked the Cannes red carpet since 2002вҖ”with a foot injury, through relentless body scrutiny post-pregnancy, with a broken arm, and through multiple other personal and professional battles we may not be privy toвҖ”there is still no writing her off. Now arriving with daughter Aaradhya Bachchan in tow, dressed in Gauri & Nainika no less, ARB remains far more than just another red carpet moment.
#Cannes #ARB #AishwaryaRaiBachchan

posts.caption:
Hi, IвҖҷm Veronica рҹ‘ӢрҹҸј a sewing pattern designer based in Melbourne, Australia.
I create patterns, tools and resources that help you sew with understanding.
My work focuses on how fabrics behave, how garments are constructed, and how sewing connects us to the history of what we wear.
Sewing Patterns
My patterns are designed for versatility, wearability and fun. They include the Hazel, Freya, Odette, Audrey, Hera, Cordelia and the Sleeve Studio вҖ” each combining comfort with thoughtful design details. YouвҖҷll also find The Noelle, a free beginner-friendly pattern that introduces my approach to fit and construction.
The Paper Doll
A print-at-home paper doll designed to help you visualise and play with my sewing patterns. You can cut it out and colour it yourself, or use the pre-filled versions featuring my fabric prints and colour palettes. The set includes curvier model options and three skin tones to choose from.
The Conscious Consumer eBook
A practical guide to fabrics, sustainability and sewing with intention. It helps you understand fibre origins, weave structures and how to make long-lasting choices when you sew or buy clothes.
Digital Sewing Tools
Two paid calculators designed for precision:
вҖў Shirring Calculator вҖ“ works out fabric shrinkage and elastic thread length.
вҖў GSM Calculator вҖ“ estimates fabric weight and drape.
Available individually or as a discounted bundle.
My PDF sewing patterns are stocked digitally through @fabricgodmother , @spotlightstores , @thefoldline , @joans.finefabric and @thedrapersdaughter .
Fabric and Accessories
You can shop my fabric designs on @spoonflower and find my illustrated phone cases at @thedairy .
Everything I create is designed to help you sew with confidence, understanding and creativity.
For all enquires email us at hello@veronicatuckerthelabel.com

posts.caption:
Lately IвҖҷve been thinking a lot about how women have been required to multitask throughout history and the impact that this has on our creativity.
On one hand, weвҖҷre able to do a million things at once and on the other hand, we have to do a million things at once.
What comes to mind when I think of this topic are usually snippets of Mr Bennet retiring to his reading room and closing the door, away from his nagging wife and wild daughters.
Imagine if, as women, we had the same luxury? And throughout history? How much further would we have come? Politically, creatively, financially.
Reading вҖҳA Room of OneвҖҷs OwnвҖҷ by Virginia Woolf really opened up my mind to this topic. I highly recommend reading.

posts.caption:
The oldest written story in human history is about a woman and what she wears.
Before Homer or the Bible or almost everything we call civilisation, the first makers were here.
This is Edition I of The First Makers: a series inside the Goddess Project tracing the weaving goddesses who presided over making across every culture, independently, without contact with each other.
This edition: Ancient Mediterranean & Near East.
Uttu. Inanna. Neith. Hathor. Isis. Athena.
Six goddesses over 5000 years of record all with one argument: making was never just craft. It was power, sovereignty, magic, and the measure of civilisation.
The Culture of Cloth podcast episode on the First Makers drops this week рҹ”— link in bio.
Save this. The series continues.

posts.caption:
вҖңI donвҖҷt want to understand everyone; I want a world where I live and let others live. In New York City, I was staying in the middle of a huge building, surrounded by people of different races and religions, and there was no tension whatsoever. Sloterdijk even talks about how we need to learn a certain kind of discretion. ThatвҖҷs what bothers me вҖ” the idea that I have to understand your world. How am I supposed to understand your world when neither you understand it nor I understand my own? IвҖҷm very superficial in that regard. You have a neighbor вҖ” establish some kind of order, with basic respect, and donвҖҷt push yourself onto them, nor let them push themselves onto you. I take great pleasure in discretion. Maybe that comes with age.вҖқ
For the May Issue of Vogue Adria, we spoke with @SlavojZizeks, one of todayвҖҷs most renowned philosophers, about the future of the left, the dangers facing modern society, one night stands, and revolution.

posts.caption:
The womenвҖҷs world number one @ArynaSabalenka understands that tennis can be opera: full of triumph and despair, grace and slapstick, sin and atonement. The 28-year-old has been at the top of her sport for the last year and a half, with a deep bench of brand associationsвҖ”including with Gucci, Nike, and Audemars PiguetвҖ”to show for it. But since her earliest days on the tour, sheвҖҷs been a fiery on-court presence, known for her outbursts (sheвҖҷs a world-class racket-smasher) and tense exchanges with officials. (She attributes much of this to being вҖңvery much a TaurusвҖқ).
Time and training have helped her to temper that fireвҖ”but it also has its uses. вҖңThis is part of my personality that can drive me crazy, but it can also drive me into that real fight mode and help me play with passion,вҖқ Sabalenka says. вҖңItвҖҷs two sides of the medal.вҖқ
For VogueвҖҷs May 2026 digital cover, @robertjhaskell meets Aryna Sabalenka in Rome to talk winning, losing, grieving, learning, and never letting up.
Photographed by @stevenkleinstudio,Written by @robertjhaskell, Styled by @tonnegood, Hair by @sandyhullett, Makeup by @markcarrasquillo, Manicure by @nailsbyetsuko, Tailoring by @sara.on.set.tailor, Produced by @thatoneproduction, Shot on Location at @faenamiamibeach. posts.caption:
On May 17, rumors broke that Shein had acquired Everlane. If it wasnвҖҷt officially confirmed three days later, it would read as satire: a brand that became popular for its commitments to radical transparency and sustainability is now under the umbrella of a brand known for its opaque practices and mass production. While the news was a shock to many customers who felt they had an accessible brand they could trust, Everlane struggled during the COVID-19 pandemic and failed to regain its footing. Private equity firm L Catterton took a majority stake in the brand in 2020; founder Michael Preysman stepped down in 2021, and leadership changes, layoffs, and debt followed.
At the link in bio, Preysman, who has spent the last year working on an electrolyte brand called Drink Magna, steps back into the world of fashion to address what happened in his own words, and reveal what he plans to do about it.

posts.caption: Is it really such filthy behavior to wear flip-flops in the city? Every time New York gets just a little bit hot, thereвҖҷs another annoying conversation about whether itвҖҷs kosher or not to let our toes out in the open, whether theyвҖҷre splayed on a rubbery pair of Havaianas flip-flops or a leather вҖңthongвҖқ sandal by The Row. This week on #Addressed, @liana_ava weighs in on whether or not "showing toe" is okay. Read more at the link in bio.

posts.caption: A red carpet is the perfect opportunity for designers to extend the runwayвҖҷs reachвҖ”but has current-season dressing reclaimed the spotlight, replacing the archival craze of recent years? Judging by this yearвҖҷs #Cannes, it certainly seems so. From Bella Hadid in custom @Schiaparelli to Demi Moore in fresh-off-the-runway @Gucci, read more about the shift away from archival fashion at the festival at the link in bio.
posts.alt_text: Photo shared by Vogue Runway on May 25, 2026 tagging @bellahadid, and @schiaparelli. May be an image of gown, dress and text.

posts.caption: The appeal of the 1920s is very much present in recent 2020s collections. It makes sense that that should be so at Lanvin, one of the houses that defined the Roaring вҖҷ20s style. Peter Copping, the creative director, talked to Vogue about how heвҖҷs accessing the Lanvin legacy in his own work and by mentoring six SCAD students who designed dresses that paid homage to the maison. Tap the link in bio to read more.

posts.caption: @AuroraJames does not pack in outfits, but she does pack in palettes. posts.caption:
@maudeapatow is ready now. Fresh off her return in the highly anticipated third season of #Euphoria, Apatow is stepping into a new chapter with the kind of clarity and control that only comes from growing up in real timeвҖ”on-screen, off-screen, and alongside co-stars who have gone on to navigate intergalactic levels of fame and careers of their own. "Just thinking about us being little kids and having no idea what was in store to nowвҖҰ It's insane," she says.
In our May cover story, Apatow opens up about the roles that shaped her, the relationships that fulfilled her, the theater work that grounded her, and the ritualsвҖ”travel, matcha, and allвҖ”that keep her moving forward. Read our full interview with the actress and director at the link in our bio.

posts.caption:
@lilamoss is having the time of her life. At just 23, Moss has already graced the cover of British Vogue, starred in a @marcjacobs beauty campaign, and walked for @coperni, @versace, @miumiu, and @victoriassecret. But her path into fashion wasnвҖҷt always a given, despite having a supermodel mother. вҖңI was always really interested in fashion,вҖқ she says. вҖңI was always putting on my momвҖҷs shoes and doing fake runways. But it was never something really that my mom wanted me to do.вҖқ
From her very first modeling opportunity, Moss knew she had found her lane. Now, sheвҖҷs using her platform to advocate for causes close to her heartвҖ”from proudly wearing her type 1 diabetes tech in editorials to collaborating on the first-ever type 1 diabetic Barbie. See how Moss is carving her own path in our summer cover story at the link in our bio.

Its high-end watchmaking workshop, @lafabriquedutemps, has collaborated with clockmaker @lepee_1839 to design the clock shaped like a soccer ball, which will be sold by SothebyвҖҷs in an online auction from June 9 to 18, with all profits donated to UNICEF.вҒ
